food_name: Burmese Pork Spare Ribs Noodle Soup
aliases: Myanmar noodle soup with pork ribs

english: Myanmar noodle soup with pork ribs

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for Burmese pork spare ribs noodle soup. FOODON_00002473 (noodle soup food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (noodle-based soup) is the closest available parent. No Myanmar-specific pork rib noodle soup entry exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this specific Burmese noodle soup dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Burmese pork spare ribs noodle soup weighing approximately 450 g, comprising broth, noodles, and pork ribs.

nutrient_source.title: Pork spare ribs, braised; noodle soup composite — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for pork spare ribs (cooked, braised) and noodle soup broth components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Burmese pork spare ribs noodle soup exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for braised pork spare ribs (NDB 10097), cooked wheat noodles (NDB 20121), and pork bone broth, combined in approximate proportions typical of a 450 g bowl (roughly 35% pork ribs, 30% noodles, 35% broth). Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Noodles, wheat-based, boiled — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to mixed noodle soup dish
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Burmese pork spare ribs noodle soup. GI of 52 is estimated using the University of Sydney GI Database value for boiled wheat noodles (GI approximately 52, low category). The pork and broth components contribute negligible glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 x 56.25) / 100 = 29.25, rounded to 29.5. Serving carbohydrate estimated at 12.5 g per 100g x 450g serving = 56.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from broth, soy sauce, and fish sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced-sodium broth, as a single 450 g bowl may contribute approximately 2160 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from wheat or rice noodles
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size carefully, as the noodle component contributes a moderate glycemic load (GL approximately 29.5 per bowl); choosing rice noodles over wheat noodles and reducing noodle quantity may help manage postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from pork spare ribs and elevated sodium from seasoned broth
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should be aware that pork spare ribs contribute saturated fat (approximately 2.1 g per 100 g) and the seasoned broth contributes high sodium, both of which may adversely affect blood pressure and lipid profiles when consumed frequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: နံရိုးမီးတောက် (romanized: Nan Yoe Mi Daw Kyat) is a traditional Burmese noodle soup dish featuring slow-braised pork spare ribs served in a rich, seasoned pork bone broth over wheat or rice noodles. The dish originates from Myanmar and is popular in Burmese restaurants in Singapore, including at Golden Cart Myanmar Restaurant. The broth is typically flavoured with garlic, ginger, soy sauce, dark soy sauce, fish sauce, star anise, and onion, producing a deeply savoury, slightly sweet, and aromatic soup. The pork ribs are braised until tender, yielding meat that falls off the bone. The noodles absorb the flavourful broth, creating a hearty and filling meal. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate protein from pork (approximately 9.8 g per 100 g), moderate carbohydrate from noodles (approximately 12.5 g per 100 g), and is relatively high in sodium due to the seasoned broth and condiments. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low category) based on the noodle component. Clinically, the dish warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension due to its high sodium content, and moderate caution for those with Type 2 Diabetes due to the noodle-derived glycemic load. The dish is not suitable for halal or vegetarian diets due to the pork content.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Burmese pork spare ribs noodle soup was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for component ingredients (braised pork spare ribs, cooked wheat noodles, pork broth) combined in proportions typical of a standard restaurant serving. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database value for boiled wheat noodles. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data (braised pork spare ribs NDB 10097, cooked wheat noodles NDB 20121, pork broth) in approximate proportions for a 450 g bowl. No direct approved-source entry exists for this specific dish. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported consistently across the component source entries. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible in braised pork and noodle components per USDA SR Legacy data. 6. GI estimated at 52 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database value for boiled wheat noodles; no direct GI measurement exists for this dish. 7. The cuisine tag 'Lor Mee' in the input source data appears to be a field survey platform tagging error; this dish is a Burmese pork rib noodle soup and is not a Singaporean Lor Mee. The tag has not been carried forward. 8. The dish contains pork and is not halal.
